title: Calciomercato no stop - Indiscrezioni, trattative e retroscena del 30 giugno
excerpt: IL MILAN ANNUNCIA RAMOS.
summary: IL MILAN ANNUNCIA RAMOS. JUVENTUS, EKHATOR ALLE VISITE. IL NAPOLI INSISTE PER GILA. GIORNATA DI SALUTI: DARMIAN LASCIA I NERAZZURRI, JUAN JESUS ED ELMAS I PARTENOPEI. LA FIORENTINA ACCOGLIE VIERY. IL COMO PRENDE MILLA E (FORSE) LIBERALI. UDINESE, LE ULTIME SUL CASO ZANIOLO L'ultimo giorno di giugno ha regalato numerosi movimenti, tra ufficialità, visite mediche e trattative che potrebbero entrare nel vivo nelle prossime ore. In attesa dell'apertura della nuova stagione, i club di Serie A continuano a muoversi con decisione per rinforzare le rispettive rose. Il Milan ha ufficializzato uno dei colpi più importanti della giornata: Gonçalo Ramos è un nuovo attaccante rossonero e ha firmato un contratto fino al 2031. Sul fronte uscite, invece, Mattia Liberali è ormai a un passo dal Como: l'accordo tra il talento classe 2007 e il club lariano è stato raggiunto e restano soltanto gli ultimi dettagli da sistemare.

title: Doppietta di Mbappé e gol di Barcola: la Francia travolge la Svezia e vola agli ottavi
excerpt: La Francia domina, batte 3-0 la Svezia e vola agli ottavi di finale del Mondiale 2026, dove affronterà il Paraguay, capace di eliminare la Germania.
summary: La Francia domina, batte 3-0 la Svezia e vola agli ottavi di finale del Mondiale 2026, dove affronterà il Paraguay, capace di eliminare la Germania. A New York i Bleus confermano la propria superiorità con una prestazione autoritaria, trascinati da un super Kylian Mbappé e da un Michael Olise ancora una volta decisivo. Il primo tempo di Francia-Svezia Nel primo tempo la squadra di Deschamps prende subito il controllo del gioco dopo il tentativo iniziale di Isak. Digne impegna Zetterstrom, Barcola crea superiorità e Mbappé si vede annullare un gol per fuorigioco. Dopo il cooling break la pressione aumenta: Rabiot sfiora il vantaggio, Mbappé colpisce il palo a porta quasi vuota e Olise centra il legno con una splendida sforbiciata. Al 45' arriva il meritato 1-0: Mbappé entra in area, salta l'uomo e trova un destro a giro potentissimo. Il secondo tempo di Francia-Svezia La ripresa segue lo stesso copione. Al 50' ci prova Tchouameni, poi al 53' Olise inventa centralmente e serve Barcola, freddo davanti al portiere per il 2-0. Il talento del Bayern continua a illuminare la manovra e sfiora anche il gol da fuori, trovando la risposta di Zetterstrom. Al 74' la Francia chiude i conti: altra giocata di Olise, arrivato a 31 assist stagionali tra club e nazionale, e Mbappé firma la doppietta personale. Nel finale Deschamps gestisce, inserendo anche Malo Gusto, Doué e Theo Hernandez. Barcola sfiora il poker, ma il 3-0 basta per certificare il dominio francese.

title: Norvegia agli ottavi dei Mondiali, finisce la corsa della Costa d'Avorio. Ora Ancelotti
excerpt: La Norvegia è tornata: 28 anni dopo, gli scandinavi tornano agli ottavi di finale di un Mondiale.
summary: La Norvegia è tornata: 28 anni dopo, gli scandinavi tornano agli ottavi di finale di un Mondiale. Battuta anche la Costa d'Avorio, la decide Erling Haaland. Partita sbloccata nel primo tempo da un grande tiro a giro di Nusa e pareggiata nella ripresa da Amad Diallo con una splendida azione personale. Alla fine il centravanti del Man City, con un facile tap-in su assist di Berga, ha chiuso la questione all'86', trovando anche il quinto gol nel torneo e portandosi a -1 da Messi. Riecco il Brasile, 28 anni dopo il regalo a Francia '98 E ora il Brasile di Carlo Ancelotti. A Francia '98 proprio la seleçao diede un aiutino alla Norvegia per agevolarle il passaggio del turno, di fatto scansandosi consapevole del primo posto già acquisito. Questa volta col dentro o fuori sarà un'altra storia. Ma quel Brasile era più forte di quello attuale. E questa Norvegia è ampiamente superiore a quella di 28 anni fa...

title: Gonçalo Ramos è un nuovo giocatore del Milan. Ed è record di spesa per i rossoneri
excerpt: È arrivata anche l'ufficialità: Gonçalo Ramos è un nuovo giocatore del Milan.
summary: È arrivata anche l'ufficialità: Gonçalo Ramos è un nuovo giocatore del Milan. Primo acquisto dei rossoneri, con la proprietà che ha subito accontentato il tecnico Ruben Amorim. Accordo fino al 2031. Il club di Via Aldo Rossi, non ha ufficializzato quanto verserà al PSG. L'acquisto più caro di sempre del Milan Di sicuro, Ramos, costerà oltre 70 milioni di euro. In ogni caso, il lusitano entrerà di diritto al primo posto nella top ten degli acquisti più costosi nella storia del Milan, superando il connazionale Rafael Leao. E a proposito, Ramos è l'ottavo calciatore portoghese della storia del Milan. Vestirà la maglia numero 9 che da Pippo Inzaghi in poi, tolto Giroud, non ha portato troppo bene. L'addio al Paris Saint-Germain In attesa delle sue prime parole da milanista, Ramos ha salutato il Paris Saint-Germain: "Insieme abbiamo scritto una storia indimenticabile. Abbiamo vinto due Champions League e numerosi altri trofei, e abbiamo condiviso momenti che rimarranno per sempre impressi nella memoria di tutti coloro che hanno fatto parte di questa avventura.

title: L'Inter saluta 4 giocatori, ma non Mkhitaryan. L'armeno rinnova e resta in nerazzurro
excerpt: Giornata particolarmente intensa in casa Inter, con 4 giocatori che hanno dato l'addio al club nerazzurro.
summary: Giornata particolarmente intensa in casa Inter, con 4 giocatori che hanno dato l'addio al club nerazzurro. Nella mattinata di martedì infatti la società ha comunicato che Yann Sommer, Francesco Acerbi, Stefan de Vrij e Matteo Darmian non avrebbero rinnovato il proprio contratto, finendo così per svincolarsi ed essere liberi di poter accordarsi con un'altra squadra. L'olandese non ha perso tempo e ha già firmato con il Panathinaikos, gli altri invece sono in attesa di una sistemazione. Discorso completamente diverso per Henrikh Mkhitaryan, che resterà invece a Milano. Più volte dopo i trofei conquistati il presidente Marotta aveva sottolineato come qualcuno avrebbe lasciato l'Inter in estate per motivi anagrafici. Chivu, eccezion fatta per il portiere svizzero, non li ha utilizzati come titolari inamovibili nella stagione conclusa, ma il loro peso all'interno dello spogliatoio era evidente, soprattutto per quello che avevano rappresentato nel recente passato. De Vrij è l'unico a cui era stato offerto il prolungamento, ma l'ex Lazio ha preferito un'esperienza in un altro campionato per misurarsi con una realtà diversa. L'Inter ha scelto di trattenere il centrocampista per tantissime ragioni, a partire da quella di natura tecnica e tattica, visto che è uno dei calciatori più intelligenti dell'intero campionato di Serie A. La sua duttilità è un qualcosa che spessissimo è tornato utile all'allenatore rumeno. Decisiva anche la sua apertura a una riduzione evidente dell'ingaggio, fi...

title: 2026 World Cup: France vs Sweden – Official Lineups: Milan duo Rabiot & Maignan face Karlstrom
excerpt: France vs Sweden is the sixth of the 2026 FIFA World Cup round of 32...
summary: France vs Sweden is the sixth of the 2026 FIFA World Cup round of 32 matches, as the pre-tournament favourites face off against a side who failed to win a single group match in their qualifying campaign for the tournament. Graham Potter’s side qualified for the play-offs due to their performance in the UEFA Nations League and took full advantage, booking their place in this summer’s tournament. Qualifying in 3rd in their group, behind Japan and the Netherlands, Sweden now face an uphill battle against one of the tournament’s strongest sides.

title: Inter defender Bastoni under investigation in Milan underage prostitution inquiry
excerpt: Inter defender Alessandro Bastoni is under investigation by prosecutors in Milan as part of an...
summary: Inter defender Alessandro Bastoni is under investigation by prosecutors in Milan as part of an inquiry into underage prostitution. According to La Gazzetta dello Sport, the 27-year-old received an avviso di garanzia, a formal notification under Italian law that a person is the subject of an investigation, and a procedural step rather than a charge or any finding of guilt, on Tuesday, and is expected to be questioned in the coming days. He is reported to be the first footballer placed under investigation in the case.

title: Juventus agree UEFA settlement and up to €20m fine as Fiorentina also sanctioned
excerpt: Juventus and Fiorentina are among the clubs sanctioned by UEFA after its Club Financial Control...
summary: Juventus and Fiorentina are among the clubs sanctioned by UEFA after its Club Financial Control Body (CFCB) concluded its assessment of teams that took part in the 2025/26 European competitions, finding a number in breach of financial sustainability rules. As per the Corriere dello Sport, Juventus were found to have broken the “football earnings rule”, which permits a maximum aggregate deficit of €60 million across the three financial years preceding the monitoring period, in this case those ending in 2023, 2024 and 2025, assessed on a three-year aggregate basis for the first time. As a result, the Bianconeri have signed a three-year settlement agreement with the CFCB, as have Newcastle United.

title: Defying logic - how Haaland could end up with 260 international goals
excerpt: Erling Haaland delivers on the biggest stage yet for Norway - but just how many goals could he end up with in international football?
summary: All five of Erling Haaland's goals at the 2026 World Cup have been one-touch finishes Erling Haaland stood on the pitch in Dallas, a goofy grin across his face and a Viking helmet atop his head at a jaunty angle. He was drinking it all in. Norway had just beaten Ivory Coast 2-1, their first knockout victory in World Cup history. It was celebrated by players and fans joining together in their now iconic Viking Row celebration - conducted by Martin Odegaard on the drum.

title: Koeman resigns, while Netherlands report racist abuse
excerpt: Ronald Koeman resigns as Netherlands manager after their World Cup exit, which led to "appalling" racist abuse aimed at players who missed penalties.
summary: Netherlands crumble in penalty shootout as Morocco reach last 16 Ronald Koeman has resigned as Netherlands manager after their World Cup exit, which led to "appalling" racist abuse aimed at players who missed penalties. Koeman's side were knocked out by Morocco in a last-32 shootout after a 1-1 draw in Monterrey.

title: Quansah and James miss England training as right-back problems mount
excerpt: Jarell Quansah and Reece James miss training as England's right-back issues mount before their World Cup last-32 match against DR Congo on Wednesday.
summary: Jarell Quansah (left) replaced Reece James against Panama for his World Cup debut Jarell Quansah and Reece James missed training as England's right-back issues mount before their World Cup last-32 match against DR Congo on Wednesday. Quansah came off with a twisted ankle in England's 2-0 win over Panama in their final group game on Saturday, with Djed Spence coming on to replace the Bayer Leverkusen defender.

title: Mbappe scores twice as France dominate Sweden
excerpt: Kylian Mbappe becomes the all-time leading goalscorer in World Cup knockout matches with after scoring twice as France crusie past Sweden 3-0 to set up a last-16 tie with Paraguay.
summary: Kylian Mbappe becomes the all-time leading goalscorer in World Cup knockout matches with after scoring twice as France crusie past Sweden 3-0 to set up a last-16 tie with Paraguay.

title: Kylian Mbappé at the double as France swat aside Sweden in World Cup masterclass
excerpt: When people form memories of the World Cup, it’s matches like this that do it. France once again won comprehensively, and against decent opposition, but the quality of their attacking play, and the sheer beauty of their goals, were difficult to fully appreciate in the moment. It’s the sort of thi
summary: When people form memories of the World Cup, it’s matches like this that do it. France once again won comprehensively, and against decent opposition, but the quality of their attacking play, and the sheer beauty of their goals, were difficult to fully appreciate in the moment. It’s the sort of thing that only comes into true focus upon reflection. Kylian Mbappé drew alongside Lionel Messi in the race for the golden boot here with another brace of unerring finishes. Michael Olise should have had a hat-trick but made do with two assists and a virtuoso performance that left jaws across the floor of the New York-New Jersey stadium. At this point it is very hard to see beyond Didier Deschamps’ men, and there is equally the sense that there is more to come. On a hot afternoon in East Rutherford, the first half served up an interesting contest with Sweden in the mood to compete and in possession of a gameplan. They had good options on the counterattack, either by releasing Anthony Elanga or going long to Viktor Gyökeres, and both would look immediately to where Alexander Isak was. The Liverpool man was released twice by his teammates in the opening 25 minutes and on both occasions got his shot off. Thanks to the close attentions of Dayot Upamecano and William Saliba, however, neither effort bothered Mike Maignan.

title: Forest Green Rovers scrap women’s team to ‘concentrate resources’ on men’s side
excerpt:
• Men’s team relegated to National League in 2024

  • Club hope women’s football will return in future

Forest Green Rovers have disbanded their women’s team for the 2026-27 season so that they can “concentrate their resources” on trying to return their men’s team
summary: Forest Green Rovers have disbanded their women’s team for the 2026-27 season so that they can “concentrate their resources” on trying to return their men’s team to the English Football League. The Gloucestershire club’s women’s side finished second in their regional division of the fifth tier of the English pyramid last term, narrowly missing out on promotion to the fourth tier by one point behind the champions, Torquay. They lost just one league match across the season. In a statement issued on Tuesday, Forest Green insisted women’s football would eventually resume at the club and they would “like it to return” in the future, but they added that their primary goal was to ensure their men’s team returned to League Two as soon as possible. Their men were relegated to the National League in 2024.

title: England and Tuchel prepare to go deep but know ignominy may lie in wait if they fail | Jacob Steinberg
excerpt:
The shadow of Iceland 2016 is all the warning England should need not to take for granted a winnable-looking first knockout game of the tournament

It was the 10th anniversary last Saturday of t
summary: The shadow of Iceland 2016 is all the warning England should need not to take for granted a winnable-looking first knockout game of the tournament It was the 10th anniversary last Saturday of the Iceland debacle. Kolbeinn Sigthórsson’s winner sliding underneath Joe Hart’s dive, Wayne Rooney chugging away in midfield, Harry Kane taking corners, and Roy Hodgson being forced to conduct a sullen farewell press conference. It all feels like something from a fever dream but no, it did happen and that really was the state England were in when their Euro 2016 campaign came to an end. Expectations could not have been lower before Gareth Southgate banished the fear. England had to take small steps before becoming contenders again, to the extent that beating Colombia on penalties in the last 16 of the 2018 World Cup and winning a knockout tie for the first time in 12 years was an achievement.

title: Ruthless Mbappe sets tone as France breeze past Sweden
excerpt: Kylian Mbappe helped book France's place in the World Cup round of 16 by taking his tournament total to six goals in a slick 3-0 victory over Sweden.
summary: Report as Kylian Mbappe scored twice to ease France into last 16, moving one behind Lionel Messi on the tournament's all-time list; the goal ties him with the Argentina star in the race for the Golden Boot this summer; France play Paraguay on Saturday for a place in the quarter-finals Kylian Mbappe helped book France's place in the World Cup round of 16 by taking his tournament total to six goals in a slick 3-0 victory over Sweden. France's procession through the group stages looked ominous for their rivals, with Mbappe starring in a show that contained a support cast just as entertaining. In New Jersey, Michael Olise proved to be the outstanding sidekick.

title: Neville: France ‘a level above’ and Mbappe ‘on verge of greatness’
excerpt: Gary Neville says France are "a level above" any other team at the World Cup after they cruised to a 3-0 win against Sweden in the round of 32.
summary: France beat Sweden 3-0 in the round of 32; Gary Neville described it as a 'statement win' with Les Bleus a 'level above' any other team at the World Cup; Kylian Mbappe scored two goals, with the Sky Sports pundit praising the forward; France face Paraguay in the last 16 on Saturday Gary Neville says France are "a level above" any other team at the World Cup after they cruised to a 3-0 win against Sweden in the round of 32. Les Bleus captain Kylian Mbappe scored twice in New York New Jersey - with the Sky Sports pundit adding the forward was "on the verge of greatness" as he went level with Lionel Messi in the tournament's Golden Boot race.

title: Rangers sign Pandur from Hull as Butland nears Tigers move
excerpt: Rangers have signed goalkeeper Ivor Pandur from Hull City in a £6m deal.
summary: Ivor Pandur, who is currently at the World Cup with Croatia, has signed a four-year deal with Rangers; Pandur moved to Hull City in 2024 and played in all but one Championship game last season, plus kept clean sheets in both play-off semi-finals and the final as promotion was secured. Rangers have signed goalkeeper Ivor Pandur from Hull City in a £6m deal. The 26-year-old - who is currently at the World Cup with Croatia - has signed a four-year deal with the Ibrox club.

title: Haaland scores late winner to set up Norway vs Brazil
excerpt: Erling Haaland scored an 86th-minute winner to send Norway to a dramatic 2-1 win over Ivory Coast - and set up a mouth-watering World Cup last-16 clash with Brazil.
summary: Match report as Norway beat Ivory Coast 2-1 to claim their first World Cup knockout-stage victory; Antonio Nusa put Norway in front but substitute Amad Diallo equalised in the second half; Erling Haaland scored a late winner and Norway now face Brazil on Sunday in the last 16 Erling Haaland scored an 86th-minute winner to send Norway to a dramatic 2-1 win over Ivory Coast - and set up a mouth-watering World Cup last-16 clash with Brazil. With a tense round-of-32 game heading towards extra-time, Norway's main man stepped up to tap in Sander Berge's cross and earn Norway their first World Cup knockout-stage win.

title: El Tenerife ‘blinda’ su sala de máquinas con Jesús Álvarez
excerpt: El pivote aragonés rescinde con el Huesca y se convierte en una nueva pieza para la medular de Cervera hasta 2028
summary: El pivote aragonés rescinde con el Huesca y se convierte en una nueva pieza para la medular de Cervera hasta 2028 El Tenerife ha iniciado el blindaje de su medular con el fichaje de Jesús Álvarez, mediocentro zaragozano de 26 años que llega para reforzar la parcela ancha del conjunto isleño. El futbolista firmará con los blanquiazules hasta el 30 de junio de 2028 tras desvincularse del Huesca, tal y como ha confirmado el club isleño a última hora de este martes. Álvaro Cervera suma así un nuevo efectivo para su línea de contención. El canterano del Zaragoza se incorpora como agente libre con el objetivo de mantener el equilibrio táctico en el regreso del Tenerife al fútbol profesional.

title: Juventus renew interest in Lecce talent Tiago Gabriel amid Bournemouth & Milan links
excerpt: Juventus have reignited their interest in Tiago Gabriel, with the Bianconeri opening a fresh round...
summary: Juventus have reignited their interest in Tiago Gabriel, with the Bianconeri opening a fresh round of contacts as they weigh defensive reinforcements for Luciano Spalletti. The Lecce centre-back is not, however, top of Juventus’ priority list, according to the reports from CalcioMercato. The club’s most pressing needs are a goalkeeper, with Michele Di Gregorio on his way out and ongoing talks with Aston Villa over Emiliano Martínez, and a striker, where efforts are focused on agreeing a deal with PSG for Randal Kolo Muani.

title: Napoli say goodbye to Elmas and Juan Jesus as contracts expire
excerpt: Tuesday marked the final day as Napoli players for both Juan Jesus and Eljif Elmas,...
summary: Tuesday marked the final day as Napoli players for both Juan Jesus and Eljif Elmas, with the pair departing as their respective situations at the club came to a close. The Brazilian defender leaves as a free agent, while Elmas returns to RB Leipzig, the club that holds his registration following his loan spell in Naples. Aurelio De Laurentiis’s side marked the occasion with warm tributes on their official channels, via Il Corriere dello Sport. Juan Jesus, who arrived in August 2021, departs after 130 appearances and a significant role in the club’s recent success.

title: Cataldi wants to stay for life as Lazio prepare new contract
excerpt: Danilo Cataldi and Lazio appear set to extend their union, with the Biancocelesti having opened...
summary: Danilo Cataldi and Lazio appear set to extend their union, with the Biancocelesti having opened talks over a new deal for the midfielder, whose current contract expires in 2027. According to Il Messaggero, via TuttoMercatoWeb, an initial meeting has already taken place between the club and Giuseppe Riso, the player’s agent, in recent days. Both parties are said to be keen to reach an agreement without any great difficulty, with Cataldi eager to continue his adventure at Formello and no urgency to finalise terms.

title: Bologna fire sale as Bournemouth, Rangers, Nottingham Forest & Juventus target stars
excerpt: Bologna are facing a busy window of outgoings, with several of their key men attracting...
summary: Bologna are facing a busy window of outgoings, with several of their key men attracting interest both at home and in the Premier League. According to CalcioMercato, the first in line to leave is Jhon Lucumí. Juventus are keen on the Colombian defender, but their proposal of €15 million plus €3 million in bonuses has been rebuffed by Bologna, who have no intention of straying from the €28 million release clause in his contract, a clause active until July 15. From England, meanwhile, come reports of interest from Bournemouth, while Nottingham Forest are said to be tracking both Lucumí and Santiago Castro.

title: Inter turn to Chelsea’s Chalobah and Liverpool’s Jones after Palestra blow
excerpt: Inter have been forced to redraw their transfer plans, with several of their early targets...
summary: Inter have been forced to redraw their transfer plans, with several of their early targets falling by the wayside. The most painful was Marco Palestra, all but secured before Chelsea swooped in, while the move for Udinese’s Oumar Solet has cooled sharply, the door left only slightly ajar. The pursuit of Liverpool’s Curtis Jones, meanwhile, has run into the Reds’ steep £35 million valuation, prompting the Nerazzurri to widen their search.

summary: Mateus Fernandes joined West Ham for £40m last summer Tottenham have agreed an £85m deal to sign Portugal midfielder Mateus Fernandes from West Ham. The north London club saw off competition from Manchester United, who were unwilling to match the fee agreed by Spurs - which is a guaranteed £85m without add-ons.

The Villans will also face a restriction on the registration of new players on their squad list for the Champions League next season.

title: Celebrations and bottle-throwing on Dutch streets after dramatic Morocco win
excerpt:
The police were pelted with bottles in The Hague but there were hugs between sets of fans in Amsterdam after a tense last-32 tie

In the Netherlands the country’s World Cup last-32 tie against Morocco was always going to be about more than football. When the Atlas Lions
summary: The police were pelted with bottles in The Hague but there were hugs between sets of fans in Amsterdam after a tense last-32 tie In the Netherlands the country’s World Cup last-32 tie against Morocco was always going to be about more than football. When the Atlas Lions dumped Ronald Koeman’s team out of the tournament in a penalty shooutout after an epic contest, joyous 6am celebrations were sparked among the Moroccan community in Amsterdam. In The Hague, on the other hand, the atmosphere turned grim. Approximately 440,000 people of Moroccan descent live in the Netherlands, and approximately 440,000 of them were asked the same question before this game: “So who will you support, then?” It was mostly good-natured, and the former manager Ron Jans displayed genuine interest when putting the dilemma to his fellow pundit Ibrahim Afellay on Dutch national television. Afellay, capped 53 times for the Netherlands, expressed and explained his support for Morocco. In the real world, the most common reaction has been one of understanding, if not sympathy, regardless of which side someone picked.

title: Mexico face up to their most terrifying opponent: the ghost of World Cup game four
excerpt:
El Tri have made a habit of qualifying from the group stage and then falling at the first hurdle. They are hoping the memory of 1986 will help end the curse

In Mexico, the phrase ya merito (“almost there”) is closely linked to the country’s men’s football team.

In Mexican Spa
summary: El Tri have made a habit of qualifying from the group stage and then falling at the first hurdle. They are hoping the memory of 1986 will help end the curse In Mexico, the phrase ya merito (“almost there”) is closely linked to the country’s men’s football team. In Mexican Spanish, it’s a colloquial, almost affectionate expression; a way of describing something that’s close enough to touch, but that can never quite be reached. Now the phrase seems to capture something more profound about Mexico’s national team – shorthand for El Tri’s habit of not exactly failing, but always just falling short.

title: LeBron to compete in 24th NBA season elsewhere with Lakers return ruled out
excerpt: LeBron James will play a record-extending 24th NBA season with a new team after it was confirmed he would not be returning to the Los Angeles Lakers.
summary: LeBron James will compete in a record-extending 24th season in the NBA with a new team after his Los Angeles Lakers exit was confirmed; James, 41, joined the Lakers in 2018 and has guided them to one NBA title in 2020 Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player LeBron James will play a record-extending 24th NBA season with a new team after it was confirmed he would not be returning to the Los Angeles Lakers.

title: Lampard signs new Coventry deal after summer interest
excerpt: Frank Lampard has signed a new contract as Coventry City head coach until 2029.
summary: Former Chelsea and England midfielder Frank Lampard has signed a deal with Coventry City; his new terms keep him at the club until 2029; Lampard led the Sky Blues to the Championship title last season and they will play in the Premier League for the first time in 25 years Frank Lampard has signed a new contract as Coventry City head coach until 2029. The former England and Chelsea midfielder penning fresh terms will be welcome news as the Sky Blues prepare for a return to the Premier League.

title: Summerville a top winger target for Man Utd but Rashford holds key
excerpt: Crysencio Summerville is emerging as a leading left-wing target for Manchester United, Sky Sports News understands.
summary: Manchester United's current transfer focus is on midfield; Crysencio Summerville's situation is said to be dependent on Marcus Rashford's situation at the club; the West Ham winger was at the World Cup with the Netherlands, before their elimination Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Crysencio Summerville is emerging as a leading left-wing target for Manchester United, Sky Sports News understands.
